instruction: Implement encode(text, shift): shift each ASCII letter by `shift` positions within its case (wrapping a-z and A-Z), leaving non-letters unchanged. encode('abc', 1) == 'bcd', encode('xyz', 3) == 'abc', encode('Hello, World!', 13) == 'Uryyb, Jbeyq!'.

files.test_caesar.py:
from caesar import encode


def test_caesar():
    assert encode("abc", 1) == "bcd"
    assert encode("xyz", 3) == "abc"
    assert encode("Hello, World!", 13) == "Uryyb, Jbeyq!"
